name: wheel rollerout
instructions: Kneel on the floor and place the wheel roller in front of you.
Place your hands on the handles of the wheel roller and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Engage your core muscles and slowly roll the wheel forward, keeping your back straight and your abs tight.
Continue rolling forward until your body is fully extended and your arms are overhead.
Pause for a moment, then slowly roll the wheel back towards your knees, maintaining control and keeping your abs engaged.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Kneel on the floor and place the wheel roller in front of you. Place your hands on the handles of the wheel roller and extend your arms straight out in front of you. Engage your core muscles and slowly roll the wheel forward, keeping your back straight and your abs tight. Continue rolling forward until your body is fully extended and your arms are overhead. Pause for a moment, then slowly roll the wheel back towards your knees, maintaining control and keeping your abs engaged. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
